svg.text: 04 - System Architecture and Runtime
What this covers: service boundaries, communication patterns, background execution, and deployment topology.
Architecture Strategy
MVP shape: modular monolith (FastAPI) with clean domain seams so services can be split later without rewrite.
Goal balance: speed-to-market now + maintainability + scale path later.
Client Layer
Web app (Next.js App Router + TS)
- SSR where needed (landing/discovery)
- rich client mode for planning dashboard and trip operations
- WebSocket for token streaming and live updates
- PWA install + push notification capability
State/data strategy
- React Query for server state + caching/invalidations
- Zustand for app-local state (auth/chat/ui/trip)
- Mapbox for route/pin/condition map rendering
Future extension
- React Native app can consume same backend contracts.
API + Service Layer
API modules: auth, users, trips, itinerary, conditions, chat, briefings, swaps, attractions.
Service modules:
AI Agent Service: prompts, LLM calls, tool orchestration, streaming.
Condition Service: fetch, normalize, cache, score, threshold alerts.
Itinerary Service: generate, validate, optimize, adapt, version.
Knowledge Service: vector indexing/retrieval + structured destination data.
External Data Service: provider adapters, rate limits, fallback/circuit breaker.
Notification Service: schedule by timezone, payload creation, delivery channel handling.
Communication patterns
REST for CRUD, WebSocket for chat stream, Celery for async/scheduled workloads.
In-process event bus (MVP)
trip.created, trip.activated, itinerary.updated, swap.accepted, briefing.generated, etc.
Migration path: external broker (Redis Streams/RabbitMQ) when coupling pressure rises.
Data + Runtime Foundations
PostgreSQL + PostGIS
- transactional source of truth for users/trips/itinerary/briefings/swaps
- geospatial indexing and proximity/path queries
Redis
- cache tiers, session/rate-limit counters, Celery broker/backend
Vector Store
- Chroma for MVP, Pinecone path for production scaling
Security baseline
- magic-link JWT (short-lived verify token + longer session token)
- authz by user ownership on all trip resources
- no secret leakage in logs, env-based secrets, HTTPS everywhere
Performance goals
REST p95 < 200ms, first chat token < 2s, condition eval < 5s, briefing generation < 30s async.
Critical Runtime Flows
Planning chat:
user action -> WS message -> AI tool calls + RAG -> streamed tokens -> state persistence -> next turn.
Morning briefing:
scheduled task -> itinerary fetch -> condition refresh/score -> optional swap eval -> briefing generation -> push + store.
Swap cycle:
degradation trigger -> multi-day candidate analysis -> recommendation payload -> user accept/decline -> itinerary update.
Deployment and Scale Path
MVP: Railway-hosted app stack + postgres + redis + worker. Keep ops simple, prioritize product signal.
Scale-up: split FE/BE, add managed DB/Redis/vector service, multi-worker queues, read replicas, CDN, horizontal API replicas.
Principle: vertical-first scaling until clear bottlenecks justify complexity.
Operational Risk Controls
- queue isolation by task type (briefings/monitoring/notifications)
- retries with bounded backoff for external API and generation failures
- health checks + key metrics: task latency, provider errors, stale data age, swap latency
- backup strategy for database + durable logs for incident forensics
Next recommended read: 05 - API, WebSocket, and Events.
